label: Apple Music Catalog by ISRC
summary: Apple explicitly documents catalog song fetch by ISRC, which makes this a strong Apple-backed SongMetadata fallback when accepted matches preserve recording identity but not a direct Apple song id.
The official response includes durationInMillis, so the metadata itself is high-quality once a trustworthy ISRC is available.
The real nuance is arbitration: Apple notes that one ISRC may return more than one song, so this lane still needs bounded provider-aware result selection.

sources.label: Apple Music API by ISRC docs

signals: Apple explicitly documents filter[isrc] on catalog songs.
The response includes durationInMillis, so the metadata itself is exactly what SongMetadata stage 1 needs.
Apple warns that one ISRC may resolve to multiple songs, so this lane is strong but not completely self-arbitrating.

notes: The adapter depends on Apple Music API access and the associated Apple developer-program terms.

entries.subject: Apple Music API by ISRC

evidence.label: Apple Music API by ISRC docs

notes: This is an official Apple hosted API path, but it requires Apple developer-token setup.

entries.subject: rryam/MusadoraKit
notes: Open-source ecosystem proof exists for Apple Music catalog integration, even though the real authority stays with Apple's service.
The metadata shape is strong, but the auth story and multi-result ISRC arbitration add real policy work.
This candidate becomes more valuable as provider-affine accepted matches preserve ISRC consistently.

methods.notes: Still possible, but auth + multi-result handling keep this less clean than a host-owned implementation.
There is no engine to port.
A native wrapper can own Apple auth while returning only normalized duration results upward.
Adds little value beyond making the auth story awkward in another place.
Too brittle for an authenticated Apple metadata flow.
Possible, but heavier than a shared host or native bridge.
Technically fine, but Node or a native bridge are cleaner first choices.
Strong path for central token ownership and result arbitration when ISRC returns multiple songs.
A CLI shell is possible but not attractive for a multi-result metadata API.
Less direct than the stronger host/bridge stories.
A remote broker can own tokens and resolve multi-result ISRC responses centrally.
Viable, but operationally heavier than the problem requires today.
Strong when Axolync later wants Apple-native adapter ownership.

notes: This is the best Apple fallback once direct Apple song ids are absent but ISRC is preserved.
It should stay below direct Apple catalog-id lookup when both are available.

openQuestions: How often will accepted SongSense matches actually preserve ISRC in a trustworthy normalized field?
Should Apple ISRC responses be allowed to compete internally, or should SongMetadata require a single unambiguous match before accepting duration?
